page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-8-lssd is a virtual machine instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 CPU architecture. It is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include any GPU accelerators, and local storage is not configured.

Operating with dedicated CPU allocation, the instance provides predictable compute performance for workloads that require stable CPU resources. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a balanced resource density, positioning it as a cost-efficient option for general-purpose workloads that do not require specialized hardware accelerators or high-capacity local storage.

This instance is suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, microservices, and development environments. The lack of integrated GPUs and local storage makes it less suitable for data-intensive databases, machine learning training, or high-performance storage-bound tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-8-lssd is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture and 32.0 GB of system memory. This resource allocation provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, offering 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not feature GPU accelerators, and local storage is not configured. With its dedicated CPU allocation, the c4a-standard-8-lssd delivers predictable performance, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for workloads requiring stable processing power without the overhead of specialized hardware. It is suited for web serving, application hosting, microservices, and general-purpose computing tasks.
